title: Christmas spice room spray
subtitle: A cinnamon, clove, and sweet orange room spray diluted in isopropyl alcohol for seasonal fragrance
excerpt: Cinnamon bark and clove bud essential oils are skin sensitisers at higher concentrations and are best used as room sprays rather than body products. At 3% total in an alcohol-based spray they are well within safe limits for air fragrance. Test on soft furnishings before spraying widely.
sourceNotes: Room spray formulation from natural perfumery and home fragrance references. IFRA cinnamon and clove sensitiser guidelines.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Make in the bottle you plan to use.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready immediately. The scent integrates slightly over 24 hours.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months in the spray bottle. Alcohol preserves the formula well. Shake before each use. Discard if the fragrance changes character significantly.

recipeTools.notes: Accurate to 0.1 g for essential oils

glossaryTerms.definition: An ingredient that can cause an allergic reaction on repeated skin contact. Cinnamon bark and clove bud essential oils are listed skin sensitisers by IFRA. They are safe for room spray use at 2 to 3% total in an alcohol base, but should not be used in body products or applied directly to skin.

A solvent alcohol used in room sprays to dissolve essential oils into a homogeneous solution that disperses evenly from a spray bottle. Using alcohol rather than water prevents the oils from floating on the surface and delivering uneven bursts of undiluted fragrance.
Room sprays typically use 2 to 5% essential oil in an alcohol-water base. For sensitising essential oils such as cinnamon and clove, keeping the total below 3% is prudent for air dispersion.

at 70% fully solubilises the essential oils and disperses them as a fine, even mist. Shake before each use. Test on a hidden area of soft furnishings first; cinnamon can stain some fabrics. Shelf life is 12 months.

content.text: Combine the essential oils with the isopropyl alcohol in the spray bottle and swirl to dissolve completely. Add the distilled water and shake to combine. Label clearly as a room spray only.

Store at room temperature. Shelf life is 12 months. Shake before each use. Spray into the room, not directly onto people, pets, or directly onto fabric without testing first. Do not use near candles or open flames; the alcohol content is flammable.
